paragraphs: I have long dreamed of a boat and sailing around the world. By education, I am a programmer and a veterinarian. I fell out of love with the vet career back when I was still studying at the Academy. I do love programming. But my true dream is the Sea, the Ocean, a boat, and a circumnavigation. And so... by the will of fate, the Universe, and a Friend who gave me the chance to crew on a yacht delivery from Tallinn to Gdynia, I finally logged my 400 nautical miles — not as a student, not as a tourist, but as a true sailor! To say I was thrilled would be a massive understatement! I stood all the night watches; we had to retreat to the bay because a storm hit us; I was hoisted up the mast to secure it. I even saw a fata morgana... I’ll return to the Tallinn-Gdynia passage in detail some other time — that’s not what this is about.

So, about the Word that was in the beginning. It just so happened that on the crossing from Hel to Gdynia, only the owner and I were left on board. We got to talking. I shared my dream: "I want to sail the world, but later. First, I need to level up my skills so I can make a living freelancing. "He said, "Andryukha, 'later' never comes! I’ve seen so many guys like that — 'I'll get my business off the ground... I'll hire a manager...' They never once set foot on a deck. If you have a dream — buy the boat! Then you'll be forced to hustle just to keep her afloat!"
It hit me like a ton of bricks. And I made my choice!
